events.title: Heber Valley Main to Main
events.description: Join us for an exciting race day! The 10K race starts at 7 a.m. from Heber City Park, taking you through beautiful Midway streets and back. The 5K race begins at 7:15 a.m. at the same location.

events.title: Wasatch Half Marathon and 10K
events.description: Come join us in the cool mountain scenery at The Wasatch Half Marathon and 10k! We are really to share this wonderful mostly trail run course to the race calendar. This event is one you don't want to miss. Starting and ending near the Soldier Hollow Nordic Center they’ll be no shortage of breathtaking scenery with the rewards of accomplishing something great!  No need for shuttles at this event as you’ll be able to park and the start/finish line.

events.title: Rock-n-Roll Train
events.description: The Rock-n-Roll Train is a 90 minute round trip ride on the historic Heber Train featuring great rock-n-roll music, trivia, and train history hosted by Mark Nelson, Executive Director. A great date night! You'll be singing along to some of the greatest rock hits ever. This year we will have a 21-and-older car with beer and wine available. You are not allowed to bring alcohol on the train! And, when the train returns you can stick around for a live concert featuring The Flashback Brothers. There will be food and drink and lots of good times at the Depot after the train! The onboard Train Bar...
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Bluebird Café Concert Series
events.description: In our beautiful outdoor amphitheater, three talented artists share both their music and the compelling stories that inspired their songwriting journeys. Doors open at 7pm, performances begin at 8pm. Performances: June- 19, 20 (Sold Out), 26, 27 (Sold Out) July- 10, 11 (Sold Out), 17, 18 (Sold Out) August- 21 (Sold Out), 22 (Sold Out), 28, 29 (Sold Out) Bench Seating is sold out for every performance. Lawn Seating is still available select dates.
events.location: Sundance Resort, 8841 N. Alpine Loop Road Sundance, Utah 84604
events.address: 8841 N. Alpine Loop Road Sundance, Utah 84604

events.title: Midway Farmers Market
events.description: The market is held every Saturday from 10:00 am to 1:00 pm at the Midway Town Square from June through October. Fresh local produce and local artisans are onsite selling goods at the farmer's market in Midway. See you there!
events.location: Midway Town Square, 200 W Main Street Midway, Utah 84049

events.title: Freedom Train
events.description: Show your Independence Day spirit with a fun ride on the annual Freedom Train. We depart on time for these patriotic 90-minute round trips to Decker Bay—great music on board. Wear your red, white, and blue! Veterans and Active Duty Personnel ride FREE (in General Admission coaches). Call the ticket office for details at 435-654-5601.
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Midway Swiss Days
events.description: The annual Swiss Days Festival takes place in Midway, Utah, the Friday and Saturday before Labor Day each year and draws a huge crowd both days. Swiss Days includes a 10K race, parade, live entertainment, and so much more. DATES: Friday, September 4 & Saturday, September 5, 2026 SCHEDULE: Friday, Sep 4th: Market 8:00 am - 8:00 pm Saturday, Sep 5th: Market 8:00 am - 8:00 pm Breakfast & 10K Run start at 7:00 am, Parade at 10:00 am PARKING: Two public parking locations offer a free shuttle bus to and from the event location. Parking is $10 and the shuttle runs approximately every 15 minutes. T...
events.location: Midway Town Square, 200 W Main Street Midway, Utah 84049

events.title: Princess and Pirate Train
events.description: Calling all Princesses, Pirates, and friends for this epic train adventure! Your 90-minute roundtrip ride includes interactive fun onboard with princesses, pirates, and hosts who will have you singing and dancing along. Each passenger receives a Twisted Sugar cookie and a juice box. Wear your princess, or pirate costume and come and have some fun!
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Wild West Days Trains
events.description: You won't want to miss this fun and family-friendly Old West Train event! The Wild West Days Trains includes a short, 45-minute round-trip train ride. On board, you'll be entertained by fun western sing-a-longs - and the Heber Valley Railroad Saloon Girls. And look out for the Old West - Heber Valley Train Gang, who might try and rob the train! At the depot, enjoy live music and the Old West Gunfight skit at the Depot pavilion. Bring the whole family and enjoy the experience.
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Red, White, and Blue Festival
events.description: Join us for a multi-day celebration of independence, family, faith, and country. Check out our lineup and join us for this annual celebration. THURSDAY, JULY 2: Heber Market on Main FRIDAY, JULY 3: Live concert with headliner, American Soldier, a tribute to Toby Keith SATURDAY, JULY 4: 5K and a 10K Main-to-Main run/walk, pancake breakfast, flag ceremony, patriotic walk, parade, evening fireworks 2026 schedule is in progress. Check back soon!
events.location: Heber City Main Street, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Summer Concerts at the Depot
events.description: Free outdoor concerts happening on select dates this summer at the Heber Valley Railroad Depot. This is the place to be for live outdoor music! Enjoy these complimentary shows. Seating provided on a first come, first served basis. Dancing is encouraged!!
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Main-to-Main 5K %26 10K Races
events.description: Kicking off the Red, White & Blue Festival, Heber Valley Chamber is hosting the fifth annual Main-to-Main 5K & 10K races, sponsored by Intermountain Health Heber Valley Hospital. This family-friendly run takes place on Saturday, July 4, 2026, at 7:00 am. Complete your registration in advance and at the link below. Same-day registration is not available.
events.location: Main Street Park Heber City, Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT
events.address: Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT

events.title: Wasatch County Demolition Derby
events.description: We're gearing up for this year's Wasatch County Demolition Derby! To see the daily heat schedule, visit destructive-behavior.com/schedule. This event will sell out. GET YOUR TICKETS IN ADVANCE. Tickets for the rodeo and the derby go on sale in early June. Please be aware of ticket scams and third-party resellers. Only tickets purchased through Wasatch County Parks & Rec will be accepted.
events.location: Wasatch County Outdoor Arena, 415 South Southfield Road Heber City, Utah 84032
events.address: 415 South Southfield Road Heber City, Utah 84032

events.title: Wasatch Coin %26 History Club Meeting
events.description: Wasatch Coin & History Club meets on the last Tuesday of each month (except December) at 6 PM in the Wasatch County Public Library, Heber City. FOR MORE INFO: Mark Avery @ 615-482-9747, averyancients.com popsavery1@gmail.com & Dr. Lee McKenzie - leefmck@gmail.com
events.location: Wasatch County Library, 465 E 1200 S, Heber City

events.title: Soulful Sundays @ Heber City Park
events.description: Join us for a serene summer evening as Soulful Sundays shares local musical talents. This enchanting hour of music will fill the Heber City Main Street Park with soothing sounds, peaceful harmonies, and community enrichment. Every Sunday will feature new artists. Bring a blanket, gather with friends and family, and experience the magic of live music in the heart of Heber City. Admission is free—all are welcome!
events.location: Main Street Park Heber City, Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT
events.address: Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT

events.title: Midway Fourth of July Celebration
events.description: Celebrate Independence Day with our small town July 4th celebration sponsored by Midway Boosters and Midway City. This family-friendly morning of festivities begins early. The evening features a stunning fireworks display. SCHEDULE: 6:00 am: Wake Up Canon Blast from Memorial Hill 7:00 am: Sunrise Service and Flag Raising Ceremony at Midway Town Square 8:00 - 10:00 am: Pancake Breakfast at Midway Town Square 10:00 am: Children's Patriotic Bike Parade Join the Red, White, and Blue Festival day-time festivities at Heber City Main Street Park, 250 S Main St, Heber 8:00 pm: Utah Symphony in Midw...
events.location: Midway Town Square, 200 W Main Street Midway, Utah 84049

events.title: Wasatch County Library Community Concert Series
events.description: Free Concert Series @ Wasatch County Library The Wasatch County Library invites you to an evening of live music. The concert is family-friendly, free to attend, and open to everyone—bring a friend and join us at the library amphitheater during the summer months or indoors during winter and inclement weather for a vibrant night of music and community!
events.location: Wasatch County Library, 465 E 1200 S, Heber City

events.title: Dominick Antonelli @ Cafe Galleria
events.description: Live Summer Music Series May through September (weather permitting) Every day but Mondays from 5:30pm – 8:30pm Sundays 10am – 1:30pm Dominick has been playing guitar for 21 years and writing original music for 15+ of those years. He looks at himself not as a guitar player, but an artist who utilizes the guitar and other forms to create an inspiring, healing, and contemplative experience for himself and listeners. Alternate tunings and intuitive playing have contributed immensely by shaping the instrumental and lyrical music that comes through him. He has a background in Integrative Health a...

events.title: Saturday Sunset Music Series
events.description: There’s no better way to spend a summer evening in Heber City than at the Saturday Sunset Music Series. Every Saturday from 7:00–8:30 PM, Main Street Park comes alive with live music, good food, and a relaxed, family-friendly atmosphere as the sun sets over downtown. Saturday programming is part of the broader Music on Main season. The series features a diverse lineup of local musicians, highlighting the depth of talent in the Heber Valley.
events.location: Heber City Main Street, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: Wine Tasting %26 Cheese Train
events.description: Step aboard for a relaxed and thoughtfully curated Wine Tasting Experience on the train, where scenic rail travel meets a guided exploration of classic wines from around the world - sponsored by Vine Lore. Guests will enjoy a five-course wine tasting, each pour selected to showcase a range of styles and regions. The experience is designed to unfold at an easy, enjoyable pace, encouraging conversation, discovery, and time to savor each wine. Each table will be presented with a generous shared cheese board, available throughout the journey for guests to enjoy alongside the wines at their leis...
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

events.title: American Soldier Outdoor Concert
events.description: A tribute to Toby Keith Live at Heber City's Main Stage. Join us for a multi-day celebration of independence, family, faith, and country. Check out our lineup and join us for this annual celebration.
events.location: Main Street Park Heber City, Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT
events.address: Main Street Park, 200 S Main St., Heber City UT

events.title: Midway America 250 Celebrations 4th of July
events.description: There’s nothing quite like music in the mountains on the 4th of July. 🇺🇸🎶 As part of Midway’s America250 celebration we welcome you to join with us for an unforgettable evening with the Utah Symphony. It will be an evening filled with music that celebrates America. 📅 Saturday, July 4, 2026 🕗 8:00 PM 📍 Midway Town Square 🎟 Free to attend Bring your blankets, chairs and your patriotic spirit-this is a night you won’t want to miss!
events.location: Midway Town Square, 200 W Main Street Midway, Utah 84049

events.title: The Motherhood Circle: Community Baby Shower
events.description: Hosted by Christian Center of Park City (CCPC) and The Hive Family Collective , the Motherhood Circle creates a welcoming entry point for moms in need who are navigating pregnancy and early parenthood. Twice per year, we will host a community-style baby shower for 50 new and expecting moms. Each event will provide parents with essential baby supplies and connect parents to trusted community organizations that support maternal health, family stability, and early childhood development.

events.title: Park City Marketing Club Workshop: Hey, what’s your story? with Paul Hochman from Humongous Media
events.description: ​In this Park City Marketing Club Workshop, Paul Hochman, co-founder of Humongous Media, will share strategies and systems to uncover your unique story and differentiate yourself from the competition. ​Workshop attendees will learn: – How to “pare away” the excess verbiage that most companies roll out when they want to say something. ​- Data-backed, proven techniques for corporate expression (tone, manner, etc.) ​- To create a video (yes, create a video) that applies the learned techniques. ​Note: Bring an iPhone/Smartphone, a notebook and a pen. ​About Paul Hochman ​Paul Hochman is the pre...
events.location: Kiln Park City, 1090 Center Dr., Park City

events.title: Knights of Columbus Blood Drive
events.description: On Friday May 29th the Knights of Columbus will be hosting a blood drive at St. Mary’s Catholic Church Gymnasium located at 1505 White Pine Canyon Road. This event will be from 12:00 PM to 6:00 PM. Please visit RedCrossBood.org and enter :KnightsPC to schedule an appointment. Thank you.
events.location: St. Mary's Catholic Church Gymnasium, 1505 White Pine Canyon Road

events.title: Graduation Bash at Junction Commons
events.description: Graduation season deserves a celebration—and Junction Commons is throwing one for the whole community! Join us for an afternoon of live music, food trucks, a climbing wall, face painting, giveaways, and more as we celebrate this year’s grads and kick off summer together. Bring your friends, bring your family, and come enjoy the energy, the sunshine, and plenty of reasons to stay awhile. Whether you’re celebrating a graduate or just ready for some weekend fun, this is your place to gather, play, and celebrate at Junction Commons.
events.location: Junction Commons, North Landmark Drive, Park City, Utah

events.title: Feel Good Spring Wellness Fair
events.description: Spring Wellness Fair with Park City Wellness Mafia – discover local experts in gut health, hormones, fitness, IV therapy, skincare & more! Feel Good Spring Wellness Fair Join us for a Spring Wellness Fair hosted by the Park City Wellness Mafia – a dynamic, female-driven collective of wellness business owners, practitioners, and experts. This inspiring group brings together a wide range of specialties, including: Acupuncture Gut health Hormone support Chiropractic care Fitness Esthetics Massage Colon hydrotherapy IV therapy Red light treatments And so much more! The vendor list includes: Pur...
events.location: Black Rock Mountain Resort Ballroom, 909 W Peace Tree Trail, Heber City, UT

events.title: Board & Donor Speaker Series: Purposeful Volunteering: Find Your Fit, Build Impact
events.description: Discover how to align your passions with meaningful service in this engaging panel discussion—moderated by Suzy Berg Lyon , featuring panelists Karen Scheible , Scott Beck , and Amy Macuga —on purposeful volunteering. Whether you’re new to giving back or looking to deepen your impact, this session will explore how to find the right opportunities, stay motivated, and make a lasting difference rooted in real needs in your community. Join us for an inspiring conversation filled with practical insights, personal stories, and actionable guidance to help you volunteer with intention and impact.
events.location: Blair Education Center, Park City Hospital, 900 Round Valley Dr, Park City, UT

events.title: Family Friendly: Wind Chimes in Clay
events.description: Create a one-of-a-kind ceramic wind chime for your home or garden. Using basic hand-building techniques, you’ll shape, texture, and decorate your clay pieces to form the chime’s body. We’ll guide you through the process, from forming the pieces to attaching the chimes for beautiful sound. You’ll learn creative glazing techniques to give your wind chime a vibrant, lasting finish. No prior experience is necessary—just bring your creativity! The price is per project, and we welcome kids under 12 when accompanied by an adult.
events.location: Kimball Art Center, 1251 Kearns Blvd, Park City, UT

events.title: Trails, Trash, & Tunes
events.description: Summit County’s Stormwater Coalition will celebrate National Trails Day (June 6) with the return of Trails, Trash and Tunes. Come and participate in a community cleanup and weed pull, enjoy live music, kids’ activities, and give back to the land in honor of National Trails Day. Free lunch for the first 50 participants! Attendees can also earn raffle tickets and win prizes by visiting the educational booths staffed by local partners and county employees. Date: Saturday, June 6, 2026 Time: 12:00 – 3:30 PM Location: Newpark Amphitheater, 1258 Center Dr, Park City RSVP FOR FREE HERE
events.location: Newpark Amphitheater, 1258 Center Dr, Park City, UT

events.title: Art Summer Camps 2026
events.description: Fill your summer with creativity at the Kimball Art Center! Explore the wide world of art in our week-long summer camps, June 8–August 14. From painting and poetry to mosaics and film, all camps are led by passionate instructors and carefully planned for each age group. Mini Artist – Ages 4-5 Kids – Ages 6-12 Tweens – Ages 10-14 Teens – Ages 13-18 See all camps and summer schedule at kimballartcenter.org/camps/
events.location: Kimball Art Center, 1251 Kearns Blvd, Park City, UT

events.title: Demonstration Garden Volunteer
events.description: Help bees, butterflies, and other pollinators by assisting with our demonstration garden in front of the EcoCenter! Volunteers will help with soil amendment, weeding, and pruning the pollinator garden in front of the EcoCenter to maintain it for the growing season. This opportunity is a great way to learn about native and waterwise plants that do well in Park City. Volunteers will receive instruction and guidance upon arrival from gardening professional Colie Belieu ( yogaearthdesign.com ). Dress for the weather and for gardening. We’ll provide the tools and supplies. Everyone is welcome, f...
events.location: Swaner Preserve and EcoCenter, 1258 Center Drive, Park City

events.title: Free Mountain Bike Event at Park City Bike and Demos
events.description: Join us for a community mountain bike ride through the trails of Park City. We’ll kick things off with a 90-minute group ride departing from Park City Bike & Demos. After the ride, stick around for pizza, drinks, and a raffle featuring prizes from Momentous, POC, and Park City Bike & Demo. Raffle prizes include: Two $200 Momentous nutrition packages POC Kortal helmet POC Devour sunglasses Park City Bike & Demos shop gear This event is free and open to all riders. Spots are limited, so be sure to reserve yours on Eventbrite.
events.location: Park City Bike & Demos, Park City, UT

events.title: Latino Arts Festival
events.description: June 12-14, 2026 | Canyons Village Forum | Friday: 4-9:30pm, Saturday: 10am-9:30pm, Sunday: 10am-6pm A multi-day celebration of Latino arts and culture, featuring live performances, visual arts, film screenings, and culinary experiences. This annual event highlights local and international artists while fostering cultural connection and community engagement.
events.location: Canyons Village, 4000 Canyons Resort Dr, Park City

events.title: American Indian Services Native Market Days
events.description: Native Market Days | July 10–11, 2026 Canyons Village | Park City, Utah Native Market Days is a signature cultural experience hosted by American Indian Services, celebrating Native art, culture, fashion, dance, storytelling, and community. Held at Canyons Village in Park City, this event brings together Native artists, performers, designers, families, community members, and visitors in a vibrant setting that honors creativity, connection, and cultural expression. This two-day event will feature a Native Market, hoop dance performances, an art competition and judging experience, community ga...
events.location: Canyons Village, 4000 Canyons Resort Dr, Park City

events.description: Help bees, butterflies, and other pollinators by assisting with our demonstration garden in front of the EcoCenter! events.title: Wasatch Back Art Festival – Heber
events.description: The Wasatch Back Art Festival is a growing community art event held in Utah’s scenic Heber Valley, showcasing a wide range of original work from local artists and makers. The festival brings together painters, photographers, sculptors, and craftspeople for a vibrant outdoor experience celebrating creativity and craftsmanship. Visitors can explore artist booths, meet creators, and enjoy a relaxed, family-friendly atmosphere that highlights the unique culture and artistic talent of the Wasatch Back region. Heber Main Street Park (250 S. Main Street) July 24th 12pm – 6pm July 25th 10am – 6pn J...
events.location: Heber Main Street Park, 250 S. Main Street, Heber, Utah

events.title: The Business Table
events.description: The Business Table is a monthly gathering hosted by Team Cheever Plumbing & HVAC intended to spark meaningful relationships among business owners in the Wasatch Back. This event is invite only. Please apply at the link below, and join us for great conversation and food at a local Park City restaurant! Sign up for our next event here!

events.title: Dominick Antonelli at Cafe Galleria
events.description: Dominick Antonelli is on his first national tour and he will be wrapping up his last official tour date in Midway, UT at Cafe Galleria on Saturday August 8th. He will be playing an evening of original music from 530-830pm. Please come out for an evening of story telling, great food and beverages, original music and to celebrate Dominick’s final stop on his first national tour. We look forward to seeing you!

events.title: Live PC Give PC
events.description: Live PC Give PC will take place on FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 6, 2026! HELP SHAPE PARK CITY’S FUTURE IN A SINGLE DAY. Every November, Live PC Give PC brings our community together through LivePCGivePC.org for 24 hours of extreme generosity. Children break open their piggy banks. Young people dip into their savings. Businesses kick in matching grants. With everyone in the community giving whatever they can, we’ve raised millions of dollars for local nonprofits. It’s how we’re working together to preserve the place we love—so it will continue to thrive for generations to come.
